builderCode:
# ── Panels Demo ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A Panel groups elements and positions them relative to its top-left corner.
# Moving the panel shifts all its children at once.
#
# Panel properties:
#   position    — (row, col) top-left corner on the grid
#   width, height — declared size in grid cells
#   show_border — True: draws a dashed border + name label
#
# Child elements use positions relative to the panel's top-left (0, 0).
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── Direct placement vs. panel placement ─────────────────────────────────────
# Three shapes placed directly on the grid at absolute positions.
Label(label='Direct placement', position=(1, 1))
Rect  (position=(2, 1), color=(239, 68, 68))
Circle(position=(2, 2), color=(59, 130, 246))
Arrow (position=(2, 3), orientation='right', color=(16, 185, 129))

# The same three shapes inside a panel.
# Their positions are relative to the panel's top-left corner (row 2, col 7).
# Try changing panel.position — all three shapes move together.
Label(label='Inside a panel', position=(1, 7))
panel = Panel(name='group')
panel.position    = (2, 7)
panel.show_border = True
pr = Rect  (color=(239, 68, 68))                       ; pr.position = (0, 0)
pc = Circle(color=(59, 130, 246))                      ; pc.position = (0, 1)
pa = Arrow (orientation='right', color=(16, 185, 129)) ; pa.position = (0, 2)
panel.add(pr, pc, pa)

# ── show_border ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# show_border=True  draws a dashed border and the panel name (from name=).
# show_border=False (default) — the panel exists but has no visible outline.
Label(label='show_border=True',  position=(6, 1))
Label(label='show_border=False', position=(6, 8))

p_shown = Panel(name='with border')
p_shown.position    = (7, 1)
p_shown.show_border = True
s1 = Rect  (color=(251, 146, 60)) ; s1.position = (0, 0)
s2 = Circle(color=(168, 85, 247)) ; s2.position = (0, 1)
p_shown.add(s1, s2)

p_hidden = Panel(name='no border')
p_hidden.position    = (7, 8)
p_hidden.show_border = False  # default — shapes are placed but panel is invisible
s3 = Rect  (color=(251, 146, 60)) ; s3.position = (0, 0)
s4 = Circle(color=(168, 85, 247)) ; s4.position = (0, 1)
p_hidden.add(s3, s4)

# ── visible property on shapes ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Any shape (inside or outside a panel) can be hidden with visible=False.
# The shape fades out but keeps its position in the layout.
Label(label='visible=True',  position=(11, 1))
Label(label='visible=False', position=(11, 8))

pv = Panel(name='all visible')
pv.position    = (12, 1)
pv.show_border = True
v1 = Rect  (color=(34, 197, 94))  ; v1.position = (0, 0)
v2 = Circle(color=(59, 130, 246)) ; v2.position = (0, 1)
pv.add(v1, v2)

pi = Panel(name='all hidden')
pi.position    = (12, 8)
pi.show_border = True
h1 = Rect  (color=(34, 197, 94))  ; h1.position = (0, 0) ; h1.visible = False
h2 = Circle(color=(59, 130, 246)) ; h2.position = (0, 1) ; h2.visible = False
pi.add(h1, h2)
